---
title: "Quick Start"
description: "Load your first dataset with data.js: one CDN script, Data.feed(), and the select()/column() methods to filter and read the result."
---

This guide walks you through loading and querying your first dataset with data.js — from a blank HTML file to a filtered result you can read values from.

## Step 1: Include the library

Create an HTML file and include data.js from the CDN — no build step, no other dependencies required for CSV/JSON:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>My First data.js query</title>
    <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/gjrichter/data.js/data.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
    <script>
        // your code goes here
    </script>
</body>
</html>
```

## Step 2: Load a dataset

The entry point for loading from a URL is `Data.feed()`. It's asynchronous — pass a callback to `.load()`:

```javascript
Data.feed({
    source: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emergenzeHack/terremotocentro/master/data/issues.csv",
    type:   "csv"
}).load(function (mydata) {
    // mydata is a Data.Table — the whole dataset is available here
    console.log(mydata.columnNames());
}).error(function (e) {
    console.error("load failed:", e);
});
```

:::{.callout-note}
## `source` has a few aliases
`Data.feed()` also accepts `src`, `url`, or `ext` in place of `source` — whichever is present first wins. `source` is the clearest name to use in new code.
:::

## Step 3: Filter and read the result

Once loaded, `mydata` is a `Data.Table`. Use `.select()` with a small SQL-like `WHERE` string to filter rows, and `.column(name).values()` to pull out a plain array:

```javascript
Data.feed({
    source: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emergenzeHack/terremotocentro/master/data/issues.csv",
    type:   "csv"
}).load(function (mydata) {
    // filter to rows where the "labels" column mentions Facebook
    var facebookIssues = mydata.select('WHERE labels like "Facebook"');

    // pull all values of one column into a plain array
    var ids = facebookIssues.column("id").values();

    console.log(ids.length + " matching issues:", ids);
});
```

That's the whole loop: load → filter → read. From here:

- [Loading Data](loading_data.qmd) covers every supported source type (JSON, GeoJSON, Parquet, GeoPackage, and more) and the three ways to load data (`Data.feed`, `Data.object`, `Data.import`).
- [Transforming Tables](transforming_tables.qmd) covers the full `select()` syntax plus `pivot()`, `aggregate()`, `sort()` and friends.
- [Remote Parquet by Bounding Box](remote_parquet_bbox.qmd) if you're working with large GeoParquet files and only want the rows in view.

---

Ready for more? Continue to [Loading Data](loading_data.qmd).
